evals.name: claude-pretooluse-guard
evals.prompt: Create a Claude Code hook that stops the agent from running destructive shell commands like rm -rf.
evals.expected_output: A PreToolUse hook (matcher Bash) reading event JSON from STDIN, blocking dangerous commands via permissionDecision:deny on stdout (exit 0) with exit 2 + stderr as fallback, keeping STDOUT clean, deliberately failing OPEN on internal errors, plus settings.json registration, a STDIN-pipe test, and pitfalls.

assertions.text: Reads the event payload as JSON from STDIN
Blocks via the PreToolUse permissionDecision:deny JSON on stdout (exit 0) as primary, with exit 2 + stderr as fallback
Keeps STDOUT clean (logs go to a file/STDERR, never to STDOUT which is the control channel)
Deliberately fails OPEN on internal errors (exits 0 on parse failure / missing dependency), and does not use set -e in a way that aborts mid-script
Registers correctly in a Claude Code settings.json hooks{} block with matcher Bash
Provides a STDIN-pipe test command and pitfalls specific to this agent/event

evals.name: claude-posttooluse-format
evals.prompt: I want a Claude Code hook that runs prettier on any file the agent edits or writes, right after it edits it.
evals.expected_output: A PostToolUse hook with matcher Edit|Write reading tool_input.file_path from the STDIN JSON, formatting that file, failing OPEN (a formatter must never block; PostToolUse can't block anyway), keeping STDOUT clean, plus correct settings.json registration, a test, and pitfalls.

assertions.text: Registers PostToolUse with a matcher covering Edit (and Write/MultiEdit) — the correct event + matcher
Reads the edited file path from the STDIN JSON (tool_input.file_path), the field that tool actually provides
Fails OPEN / exits 0 (a formatter must not block; PostToolUse cannot block the already-completed tool anyway)
Keeps STDOUT clean (logs to file/STDERR, not the protocol channel)
Provides settings.json registration, a test command, and pitfalls

evals.name: claude-stop-notify
evals.prompt: Make a Claude Code hook that sends me a desktop notification when the agent finishes responding.
evals.expected_output: A Stop hook reading the STDIN JSON, firing a desktop notification, always exiting 0 (observer/notifier — must never block the agent stopping), keeping STDOUT clean, plus settings.json registration, a test, and pitfalls.

assertions.text: Targets the Stop event and reads the event JSON from STDIN
Is an observer/notifier that fails OPEN — always exits 0 and never blocks the agent from stopping
Keeps STDOUT clean (no protocol-channel pollution; logs to file/STDERR)
Registers correctly under Stop in settings.json and provides a test command + pitfalls

evals.name: cursor-block-env-research
evals.prompt: Write a Cursor hook that blocks the agent from reading or editing my .env files.
evals.expected_output: A Cursor hook that uses CURSOR'S actual contract (not Claude Code's): registered in a Cursor hooks.json, on the right Cursor event (e.g. beforeReadFile / preToolUse / beforeShellExecution), denying via Cursor's stdout JSON {"permission":"deny"} (and/or exit 2), accounting for Cursor's fail-open-by-default (failClosed:true for a real gate), reading the file path from the event, plus install/test and pitfalls. The tell of research: it does NOT use Claude Code's settings.json or hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision shape.

assertions.text: Uses Cursor's real registration (a Cursor hooks.json), NOT Claude Code's settings.json hooks{} block
Uses Cursor's deny contract — stdout JSON like {"permission":"deny"} (and/or exit 2) — NOT Claude Code's hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision shape
Targets a correct Cursor event for file access (e.g. beforeReadFile / preToolUse / afterFileEdit / beforeShellExecution), and matches/denies .env paths
Accounts for Cursor's fail-open-by-default for a security gate (e.g. sets failClosed:true or explicitly notes it)
Reads the event JSON from STDIN and keeps STDOUT clean (logs to STDERR/file)
Provides Cursor-correct registration, a test, and pitfalls (i.e. shows evidence of researching Cursor's current contract, not reusing Claude Code's)

evals.name: windsurf-block-forcepush-research
evals.prompt: I use Windsurf — write a hook that blocks the agent from running `git push --force`.
evals.expected_output: A Windsurf hook using WINDSURF'S actual contract: registered in a Windsurf hooks.json on pre_run_command, reading the JSON event from STDIN, and blocking via EXIT CODE 2 ONLY with the message on STDERR — recognizing Windsurf has NO stdout decision channel (so it must NOT emit a stdout JSON permission/decision object expecting it to block). Includes a Windows powershell variant note, install/test, and pitfalls. The tell of research: exit-2-only blocking, not a Claude-Code-style stdout JSON decision.

assertions.text: Blocks via EXIT CODE 2 only (Windsurf's contract), with the block message on STDERR — does NOT rely on a stdout JSON decision object to block
Targets the correct Windsurf event (pre_run_command) and reads the JSON event from STDIN (tool_info.command_line or equivalent)
Registers in a Windsurf hooks.json (e.g. .windsurf/hooks.json), NOT Claude Code's settings.json
Does NOT emit a Claude-Code-style stdout JSON permission/decision and assume it blocks (recognizes Windsurf has no stdout decision channel)
Matches git push --force / -f and notes a Windows powershell variant (Windsurf supports a separate powershell command)
Provides registration, a test command, and pitfalls (evidence of researching Windsurf's exit-code-only contract)
